---
title: "Baseball Pitch Trajectory Simulator"
subtitle: "See how any pitch moves through space — from release to the catcher's glove"
---

::: {.hero-section}

A free, browser-based tool that simulates the 3D trajectory of a pitched baseball using real physics.
Search any MLB pitcher, pick a pitch, and watch it fly.

[**Launch the Simulator**](https://baseball.skill-vis.com){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg target="_blank"}
&nbsp;&nbsp;
[Quick Start Guide](getting-started.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-lg}

:::

![The simulator in action — Ohtani's fastball trajectory with spin and no-spin comparison](images/hero.png){fig-alt="Simulator showing a pitch trajectory with orange spin path and cyan no-spin path"}

## What It Does

You type a pitcher's name. The simulator pulls their actual pitch data from MLB Statcast — every four-seam fastball, slider, curveball, and changeup they threw. Data is available from **2016 to the current season** (default: 2025). Pick any pitch, hit Simulate, and watch the ball travel from the pitcher's hand to home plate in 3D.

The trajectory is not an animation based on video. It is a **physics simulation**. The ball's path is computed from its release speed, spin rate, and spin axis using the same aerodynamic model developed by [Prof. Alan Nathan](https://baseball.physics.illinois.edu/) at the University of Illinois — the standard reference for baseball trajectory research.

## Why It Matters

::: {.feature-cards}

::: {.feature-card}
### For Pitching Coaches
Overlay a pitcher's fastball and changeup to see if they tunnel effectively. See exactly where and when the two trajectories diverge.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### For Analysts
Compare spin decomposition (backspin, sidespin, gyrospin) across pitchers. Export full trajectory data — position, velocity, acceleration, and force components — to CSV for further analysis.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### For Player Development
Enter Rapsodo measurements directly and simulate the resulting trajectory. Test "what if" scenarios by adjusting spin rate or axis.
:::

:::


## Key Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **MLB Statcast Integration** | Search any pitcher, browse their game-by-game pitch data, simulate any individual pitch |
| **Multiple Input Modes** | Statcast data, manual sliders, or Rapsodo measurements |
| **Overlay Comparison** | Stack multiple pitches to compare trajectories side by side |
| **Batter's Eye View** | First-person camera from the batter's box — see what the hitter sees |
| **Pitch Tunnel** | Visualize the tunnel point (23.8 ft) where the batter must decide |
| **Spin Axis on the Ball** | Animated baseball with procedural seam texture, rotating at real angular velocity |
| **CSV Import / Export** | Full trajectory data with velocity, acceleration, and force breakdown |
| **Video Recording** | Export the 3D view as MP4 |

## Pitch Types at a Glance

The simulator uses the same pitch type classification and color coding as MLB Statcast:

<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-ff">FF</span> 4-Seam Fastball &nbsp;
<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-si">SI</span> Sinker &nbsp;
<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-fc">FC</span> Cutter &nbsp;
<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-sl">SL</span> Slider &nbsp;
<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-cu">CU</span> Curveball &nbsp;
<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-ch">CH</span> Changeup &nbsp;
<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-fs">FS</span> Splitter &nbsp;
<span class="pitch-type-badge pt-st">ST</span> Sweeper


## How the Physics Works

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="For the curious (optional)"}

The simulator solves the equations of motion for a spinning ball in air:

- **Drag force** slows the ball down (opposing its velocity)
- **Magnus force** pushes the ball sideways and/or upward, perpendicular to both the spin axis and the velocity
- **Gravity** pulls the ball down

The spin is decomposed into three components:

- **Backspin** — creates "rise" (resists gravity). A four-seamer with 2200 rpm backspin drops about 40 cm less than a ball with no spin.
- **Sidespin** — creates horizontal movement. A slider's lateral break comes from sidespin.
- **Gyrospin** — spin around the direction of travel (like a bullet). Does **not** create movement. A pitch with high gyrospin and low backspin/sidespin will have less movement despite high total spin rate.

The simulation uses 4th-order Runge-Kutta integration with 0.001-second time steps, matching the accuracy of Nathan's original trajectory calculator.
:::

## Credits

- Physics model: [Alan Nathan's Trajectory Calculator](https://baseball.physics.illinois.edu/)
- Pitch data: [MLB Statcast](https://baseballsavant.mlb.com/) via Hawk-Eye tracking
- Baseball seam curve: [mathcurve.com](https://mathcurve.com/courbes3d.gb/couture/couture.shtml) parametrization
